name: bean
description: A Codex /bean skill for running large tasks as a recursive convergence loop. Full enforcement comes from the installed bean runtime: bean-check plus the native Codex Stop hook.

interface.shortDescription: Explicit /bean recursive convergence loop for careful tasks
interface.longDescription: bean is a provider-neutral skill that runs a task as a recursive convergence loop: investigate the most decisive open question, record what you learn as typed evidence in a ledger, compile to score what is still weak or contradictory, revise beliefs the evidence overturns, and loop until it converges. In full 2.0 install mode, ./install.sh builds the static runtime binaries and registers the native Codex Stop hook so an active .bean ledger blocks premature completion until bean-check converges. In Codex plugin-only mode, /bean still provides the loop discipline, but enforcement is advisory until the runtime binaries and hook are installed. No network calls or telemetry are added by bean itself.
defaultPrompt: Use /bean to investigate, record evidence, and loop until the answer converges.
Use /bean to revise beliefs as new evidence overturns earlier claims.
Use /bean to open a cross-model blindspot review on high-stakes work.
